questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 58° and angle B measures 67°. What is the measure of angle C in degrees?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 58° − 67° = 55°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one acute angle measuring 34°. What is the measure of the other acute angle in degrees?
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary (sum to 90°). The other acute angle = 90° − 34° = 56°.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, the vertex angle (the angle between the two equal sides) measures 42°. What is the measure of each base angle in degrees?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. If the vertex angle is 42°, then 2x + 42° = 180°, so x = 69°.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the angles formed at the first intersection is 123°. What is the measure of the corresponding angle at the second intersection?
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles formed when a transversal crosses parallel lines are equal. The corresponding angle also measures 123°.

questions.question: In triangle XYZ, the exterior angle at vertex Z measures 118°. If angle X measures 52°, what is the measure of angle Y in degrees?
questions.explanation: An exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. So 118° = 52° + angle Y, giving angle Y = 66°.

questions.question: A triangle has angles in the ratio 2:3:4. What is the measure of the largest angle in degrees?
questions.explanation: Let the angles be 2x, 3x, and 4x. Then 2x + 3x + 4x = 180°, so 9x = 180°, x = 20°. The largest angle is 4x = 80°.
